questions.question: How does habitat fragmentation affect red fox populations?

options.A: It reduces territory and food access

questions.correct_feedback: Fragmented habitats limit resources and increase competition.

questions.question: Which species is most at risk due to slow movement and long lifespan?
questions.correct_feedback: Slow movement and long lifespan make it hard for turtle populations to recover quickly.

questions.question: Which temperate forest species benefits from conservation laws that protect large habitats?
questions.correct_feedback: Protected forests help maintain stable bear populations.

questions.question: Which threat affects all three species black bear red fox and eastern box turtle?
questions.correct_feedback: Human development reduces forest habitats for all these species.

questions.question: Why are Eastern box turtles especially vulnerable to population decline?

options.A: Low reproduction rates and high mortality

questions.correct_feedback: They reproduce slowly so populations cannot recover quickly from losses.
